screenshot_note: Puppeteer/Chromium could not launch in this environment due to thread resource limits (pthread_create: Resource temporarily unavailable). Code analysis performed against /workspace/src/ source files. Visual reference cross-checked against qa26 and qa27 screenshot artifacts on disk.

fixed_since_last_review.title: Vibe Sparkline Has No Legend or Context
fixed_since_last_review.resolution: A hover legend showing the last 3 vibe-state pills was added via .sparkline-legend and .sparkline-legend__pill. The underlying sparkline already had per-circle <title> tags. Partially complete — see new issue about touch-device inaccessibility.

suggestions.title: Stats Bar 5-Column Fixed Grid Still Not Fixed (Regression Carry #27)
suggestions.description: App.css line 118 still reads `grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr)`. The StatsBar renders 5 base cards plus up to 2 optional cards (error count, stuck workers), totalling 7 cells in a 5-column grid. The overflow silently wraps to an orphaned second row. This was the top-priority bug from review #27 and has received zero code changes. Fix: change to `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(160px, 1fr))`. One line. Zero risk.

suggestions.title: Inter Font Still Not Loaded — Carry #27
suggestions.description: index.html still has no Google Fonts <link> or @import for Inter. The root in index.css declares `font-family: 'Inter', 'Segoe UI', system-ui` but Inter is never fetched. Windows users silently render in Segoe UI, Linux in whatever `sans-serif` resolves to. Thirteen reviews have passed since this was first flagged. Add one preconnect + stylesheet link to Google Fonts, or `npm install @fontsource/inter` and import it in main.jsx. Takes 5 minutes. Do it.

suggestions.title: Mobile Office Floor: Names Gone, Workers Anonymous at 480px
suggestions.description: Office.css now applies `.char__name { display: none }` at 480px (≤480px breakpoint), which hides worker names entirely instead of the sub-pixel 0.32rem text flagged in review #27. This is arguably worse — the fix traded illegibility for invisibility. At 375px mobile, workers are now completely anonymous blobs. Between 481px and 600px, names still render at 0.44rem (~7px). Neither state is acceptable. Required: either a mobile-specific list/grid view that replaces the isometric layout at ≤640px, or a tap-to-reveal tooltip with worker name/status. The isometric floor is a desktop-first visualization; pretending otherwise isn't design, it's avoidance.

suggestions.title: NEW: Fetch-Warn Retry Button Missing isSyncing Guard
suggestions.description: The `fetchWarn` banner's Retry button (rendered when one of the two parallel fetches fails) calls `syncFromGitHub` with no `disabled` prop and no `isSyncing` check. Compare with the `sync-now-btn` in the header which correctly disables when `isSyncing` is true. A user seeing 'Some data may be stale' who clicks Retry rapidly can fire multiple concurrent fetches — each calling `Promise.allSettled([fetchWorkersFromGitHub(), fetchActivityFromGitHub()])` simultaneously, causing race conditions on `setAllWorkers`, `setRoster`, and `setActivityLog`. Fix: add `disabled={isSyncing}` to the Retry button in the warn banner (and the error banner, same issue).

suggestions.title: NEW: Footer Quote Counter Modulo Logic Is Broken
suggestions.description: The footer quote counter shows `(quoteBonus % (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)) + 1 / (BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1)`, e.g. '1/9 … 9/9' cycling every 9 clicks. But `showBonus` TOGGLES on every click — alternating between the vibe tagline and bonus taglines every 2 clicks, not every 9. The counter resets to '1/9' at click 9 while `showBonus` is true and the user sees a bonus tagline, making the counter completely misaligned with visible content. It also shows '9/9' when the vibe tagline is displayed (showBonus=false), which is wrong. Simplest fix: track a separate `quoteIndex` for bonus and show `${quoteIndex + 1}/${BONUS_TAGLINES.length}` only when `showBonus` is true, or remove the counter entirely.

suggestions.title: Escape Key Still Does Not Clear Active Tag Filter — Carry #27
suggestions.description: The global keydown handler at App.jsx line 765 still reads `else if (e.key === 'Escape') document.activeElement?.blur()`. When `selectedTag` is set, pressing Escape should also call `setSelectedTag(null)`. This is standard filter-UI keyboard behavior (see: Notion, Linear, GitHub Issues). A keyboard-only user has no way to clear the tag filter without a mouse click. One-line fix: `else if (e.key === 'Escape') { document.activeElement?.blur(); setSelectedTag(null); }`. This was marked medium-priority in review #27 and has had zero changes.

suggestions.title: completionToast Still Fully Inline-Styled — Carry #27
suggestions.description: The '🎉 X shipped it!' toast is still rendered with a dozen inline style properties: `background`, `border` (hardcoded hex color), `borderRadius`, `padding`, `fontSize`, `fontWeight`, `color`, `boxShadow` (hardcoded rgba), `zIndex`, `whiteSpace`, `pointerEvents`, and `animation`. Every other notification element in this app uses CSS classes. Create a `.completion-toast` class and a `--toast-accent` CSS custom property. Pass only `style={{ '--toast-accent': completionToast.color }}` as the inline override. This has been open since review #27.

suggestions.title: No aria-live Announcement When Data Syncs — Carry #27
suggestions.description: The `isSyncing` and `lastSynced` state are both tracked. The sync status card in StatsBar updates visually when data arrives. But there is still no `aria-live='polite'` region announcing the outcome to screen readers. Add a visually-hidden `<span aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' className='sr-only'>` that renders `'Dashboard updated, ${activeWorkers.length} workers'` after each successful sync (triggered by the `lastSynced` state changing). The polling-paused pill now uses aria-live, showing the team knows the pattern — apply it to sync completion.

suggestions.title: Activity Log Worker Filter Shows Roster Workers Only — Carry #27
suggestions.description: The new worker filter pill bar (a welcome addition this cycle!) still derives its options from `roster.map(member => ...)`, which only includes workers listed in data/workers.json's `roster` array. System events by 'Bloberto', events by decommissioned workers, or workers present only in activity.json are silently unfiltered. Fix: derive filter options from `Array.from(new Set(activityLog.map(e => e.worker).filter(Boolean)))` and merge with roster, deduplicating by name. This ensures every worker name that actually appears in the log gets a filter pill.

suggestions.title: App.jsx and Office.jsx Grew Again — Carry #27
suggestions.description: App.jsx grew from 1,273 to 1,285 lines (+12). Office.jsx grew from 1,417 to 1,447 lines (+30). The new solo-spotlight layout, fetchWarn banner, polling-paused pill, and worker filter pills were all added inline rather than extracted. This is the third consecutive review flagging this. Each new feature added to these monoliths increases cognitive overhead and collision risk. Concrete next step: extract `StatsBar`, `ActivityLog`, `ActivityLogEntry`, `WorkerCard`, `VibeSparkline`, `ShortcutToast`, and `LoadingSkeleton` from App.jsx into `src/components/`. These are already discrete, named functions — the extraction is mechanical.

suggestions.title: NEW: Sparkline Legend Is Hover-Only — Invisible on Touch Devices
suggestions.description: The new `.sparkline-legend` element (added this cycle to address review #27's 'no context' issue) is controlled by `.stat-card:hover .sparkline-legend { display: flex }`. On touch devices there is no hover state, so the legend is permanently hidden — the fix is inaccessible on the platform (mobile) where accessibility help is most needed. Fix: add `@media (hover: none) { .sparkline-legend { display: flex; } }` in App.css to always show the legend on touch devices. The SVG `aria-label` text is adequate for screen readers but sighted touch users still can't see the legend.

suggestions.title: NEW: polling-paused-pill Uses Conditional aria-live (Unreliable)
suggestions.description: The `polling-paused-pill` span uses `aria-live='polite'` but the element is conditionally rendered (`{pollingPaused && ...`). An `aria-live` region must be present in the DOM before its content changes to reliably announce to screen readers — elements that are added to the DOM with content already set will not announce in all screen reader/browser combinations. Fix: always render the span but toggle its content: `{pollingPaused ? '⏸ Auto-refresh paused. Move mouse to resume.' : ''}`. The visible pill can remain conditional.

suggestions.title: getTaskTags() Still Called Twice Per Worker Per Render — Carry #27
suggestions.description: At every render of the workers grid, `getTaskTags(w.task)` is called once at the render site to compute `isDimmed` and once again inside WorkerCard to render the tag pills. With 6 workers and a selectedTag active, that is 12 regex evaluations per render cycle (6 patterns × 2 calls × 6 workers = 72 pattern tests). Compute tags once and pass as a prop: `const tags = getTaskTags(w.task)` at the call site, then `isDimmed={selectedTag !== null && !tags.some(t => t.label === selectedTag)}` and `tags={tags}` prop on WorkerCard. WorkerCard already receives the tags indirectly — this is pure redundancy.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS Block 'Mobile Header Fix (Luna Review #41)' Still Present — Carry #27
suggestions.description: App.css lines 1099–1117: the `/* === Mobile Header Fix (Luna Review #41) === */` block is still there. It targets `.tab-content` (no JSX match), `.header-subtitle` (no JSX match), and `.tab-toggle margin: 0.3rem 0` (duplicates existing .tab-toggle rules). The `.header h1` and `.header-subtitle` rules are dead selectors from a removed design. Additionally, the Office.css `@media (max-width: 480px)` block at line 2250 contains `.bloberto-character__name { display: none }` which is a ghost selector — the actual class is `.char__name`. Audit and purge dead selectors.

suggestions.title: Version String Still v1.0.0-chaos — Carry #27
suggestions.description: The footer still shows `v1.0.0-chaos` through 28 reviews, including additions of: vibe sparklines, isometric office floor, worker confetti, activity log with type + worker filters, solo spotlight layout, polling pause detection, hash-based routing, and custom SVG character avatars. This communicates stasis to returning users and makes it impossible to reason about 'what changed'. At minimum bump to `v2.0.0-chaos`. Ideally adopt a date-based scheme: `v2026.03-chaos`. It's one string.

summary: Review #28 — a net regression from #27's 4.4 down to 4.0, and honestly, that's being charitable. Three high-priority issues from last review sit completely untouched: the stats bar 5-column overflow, the missing Inter font, and mobile office unusability (which got 'fixed' by deleting worker names entirely at 480px — congratulations, the names are no longer illegible because they no longer exist). Zero of review #27's medium-priority issues were resolved. The files actually grew. Two new bugs arrived: the fetch-warn Retry button can fire concurrent fetches because it's missing `disabled={isSyncing}`, and the footer quote counter has a modulo logic mismatch that makes it display wrong positions relative to what's shown. On the positive side: the new worker filter pills in the activity log are genuinely useful (roster-only data source notwithstanding), the solo-spotlight layout for single workers is a lovely polish moment, and the fetchWarn partial-failure banner shows thoughtful resilience engineering. The sparkline legend from last review's suggestion was implemented, but it's hover-only with no touch fallback, which is the QA equivalent of fixing a leaky roof by putting a bucket under it. The fun factor and visual design remain elite — this codebase has taste in abundance. What it needs is a dedicated 'fix the backlog' sprint before adding more features. At current trajectory, review #29 will be measuring 1,300-line App.jsx additions against a list of 15 unresolved issues. Ship it? Yes. Proud of it? Ask again after the sprint.
